name: Vegetable Soup (Mcdougall)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Tamari Soy Sauce, low sodium
1 cup String Beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 10 cups of water in a large pot.
2. Add the chopped potatoes and cook until tender.
3. Add the remaining vegetables (carrots, onions, zucchini, tomatoes, garlic) and cook for a few minutes until they begin to soften.
4. Stir in the tamari soy sauce, basil, thyme, onion powder, oregano, cumin, dill weed, and parsley.
5. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer about 45 minutes.
6. Variations: Use 2 cups tomato juice instead of water for added flavor.
7. About 15 minutes before the end of cooking time, add cooked grains or spaghetti noodles (such as brown rice, barley, or whole wheat spaghetti) to the pot.
8. Additional vegetables can be added during the last 5 minutes of cooking for extra nutrition and flavor, such as peas, corn, green pepper, leeks, mushrooms, or spinach.
